blocks.text: DTC P2135: Throttle opening sensor 1/2 correlation error

The DBW system opens and closes the throttle valve under electronic control. The system consists of a throttle actuator built into the throttle body, throttle valve and throttle position sensor 1/2, accelerator pedal position sensor 1/2, DBW relay, and PGM-FI ECU. When the accelerator pedal is operated, the accelerator pedal position sensor is driven to detect the amount of depressing the accelerator pedal. The throttle valve opening target value is set based on the amount of depressing the accelerator pedal signaled by the accelerator pedal position sensor, and the throttle actuator is driven to open and close the throttle valve to achieve the target value. The actual throttle valve opening is detected by the throttle opening sensor 1/2 attached to the throttle body. If the difference between the throttle opening degree sensor 1 opening degree and the throttle opening degree sensor 2 opening degree is compared and the difference continues below the set value for more than the set time, the PGM-FI determines that a short circuit failure has occurred between the throttle opening degree sensor 1 and throttle opening degree sensor 2, and if the difference continues above the set value for more than the set time, the PGM -FI ECU judges that a correlation error has occurred in the throttle opening degree sensor and stores the DTC.

blocks.text: Execute the DBW test in the Inspection menu with HDS.
If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
